frameworks.name: Structured Event Memory (SEM)
frameworks.one_line: neuro-symbolic generative model of event dynamics over symbolic scenes embedded in vector space

frameworks.name: probabilistic generative model
frameworks.one_line: probabilistic reasoning over event dynamics supports segmentation, schema learning, and reconstruction

frameworks.name: neuro-symbolic hybrid
frameworks.one_line: combines structured symbolic scene representations with neural network continuous dynamics

core_question: How do humans segment continuous experience into discrete events and use learned event structure to generalize and reconstruct memory?
key_assumptions: experience can be carved into discrete events with latent schemata
scenes admit a structured symbolic representation embeddable in a vector space
event boundaries and schemata are inferable via probabilistic reasoning
manipulations: input modality scaled from low-dimensional symbolic scenes to naturalistic high-dimensional video
event schema reuse versus instantiation of new schemata
key_findings: SEM produces human-like event segmentation on naturalistic video data
model accounts for a wide array of human event-memory phenomena
model scales up to high-dimensional input spaces
interpretations: combining symbolic structure with neural embeddings captures human event cognition better than either alone
event segmentation, schema learning, and memory reconstruction emerge from a single generative inference process
